source.section: Part 9 - Radar & Sensors > 5 - JHMCS (Joint Helmet-Mounted Cueing System) > 5.2 - JHMCS Power-Up & Alignment

steps.action: To equip the JHMCS (Joint Helmet-Mounted Cueing System) helmet, you have to contact the ground crew. Press “\” (communication menu binding) to contact ground crew Press “F8” to select “Ground Crew” Press “F8” to “Change helmet-mounted device”. Press either “F1” for JHMCS or “F2” for NVGs.
Turn HMD brightness knob to BRT (Bright).

Perform HMD (Helmet-Mounted Display) BIT (Built-In Test). Go in the SUPT (Support) page. Press OSB next to BIT (Built-In-Test). Press OSB next to DISPLAYS (NOT RDY). Press on OSB next to HMD to start Helmet-Mounted Display Built-In Test. “PBIT GO” indicates that the test is ready to be run.

Wait until the HMD test is finished. The IN TEST indication will first blink on your helmet. While test symbology cycles on your helmet display, the BIT page will indicate “HMD IN TEST”. When test symbology starts showing the same pictures, the test can be considered complete.
Stop the HMD test by pressing the OSB next to “STOP”. The BIT page will then indicate “HMD GO”.

Perform Coarse Helmet Display Alignment. Go in the SUPT (Support) page. Press OSB next to HMD (Helmet-Mounted Display). Press OSB next to ALIGN to start JHMCS alignment. “READY” indication will then appear on your helmet cross. Move helmet cross on the alignment reference cross of the Heads-Up Display (HUD). When helmet cross and HUD cross are roughly aligned, press and hold the Cage/Uncage Button (“C” binding) for more than 2.5 sec while keeping both crosses aligned. While the alignment process is taking place, the “ALIGNING” indication is visible. The “ALIGN OK” indication appears when the alignment is complete. You can then release the Cage/Uncage button.

If required, perform Fine Helmet Display Alignment. The coarse alignment may not be perfect and may require a fine adjustment (FA). The “FA DX/DY” indication means that an adjustment is needed for the horizontal and vertical axis. (Throttle Designator Controller) AFT/FWD/LEFT/RIGHT controls to align the upper small cross with the HUD reference cross. Press the Cage/Uncage button to select the roll fine adjustment (FA ROLL). (Throttle Designator Controller) LEFT/RIGHT controls to align the lower small cross with the vertical line of the HUD reference cross. When fine alignment is satisfactory, press the OSB next to ALIGN to unbox it. This will save the alignment.
The default setting for the JHMCS is that the HMD overlay will only be visible when looking away from the Heads-Up Display.

Press Sensor Control Switch FWD to toggle the TDC (Throttle Designator Controller) priority between the HUD (Heads-Up Display) and the HMD (Helmet-Mounted Display). TDC priority is assigned to the helmet when the aiming reticle appears on the helmet. A center dot on the HUD’s TVV means the priority is assigned to the HUD.
Select either A/A Master Mode for using the JHMCS with air-to- air missiles or A/G Master Mode to designate ground targets with the helmet.
To manually “blank” (occlude) the Helmet-Mounted Display, press the Recce Event Marker button on the stick (“R” binding). When enabled, all HMD symbology is hidden.
